title: Route-reflector VPNv4 session flap after a core link fault
symptom: Bursty bgp_update_rate and bgp_withdraw_rate on the rr1<->pe-dc1 VPNv4 session, rising bgp_flap_penalty, adjacency_flap_count incrementing, and an OSPF SPF storm in the core.
root_cause: An intermittently failing core link caused the underlay next-hop to flap, which oscillated the BGP best-path A->B->A and propagated withdraw/re-advertise churn through the route reflector to all PEs.
contributing_signals: ospf_spf_rate rising as the IGP reconverged repeatedly
resolution: Enabled BGP route-flap damping on the affected VPNv4 address-family and installed a static fallback for the critical corporate prefix; churn fell to baseline and the table stabilised within 5 minutes. The failing core link was then repaired and damping relaxed.

timeline.event: Route-flap penalty + CUSUM/Page-Hinkley fire on update-rate; graph correlation flags rr1 as origin.
Granger ranking identifies pe-dc1 peer / corporate prefix as the flapping source.
Damping + static fallback approved and applied via NAPALM.
Update/withdraw rates back to baseline; routing stable.
